title: Two structured output modes: JSON outputs vs strict tool use

body.concept: Claude offers two ways to guarantee structured output. (1) JSON outputs via output_config.format: forces Claude's entire response to be valid JSON matching a JSON Schema you provide. Use this when you want Claude to return data directly — no tool calling involved. (2) Strict tool use with strict: true on a tool definition: guarantees the tool_input object matches the tool's input_schema exactly. Use this when Claude is calling tools and you need the arguments to be schema-valid. Both use 'constrained decoding' — the model is physically prevented from emitting tokens that would violate the schema, so compliance is 100%, not best-effort.
body.analogy: Think of a form-filling kiosk vs a vending machine. JSON outputs (output_config) is the kiosk: you design the form, and the user can only type valid data. Strict tool use is the vending machine: you define which buttons exist and what each accepts, and the machine only allows valid button presses.
body.visual: Two parallel paths: Path A (JSON outputs): Request with output_config.format + JSON Schema → Claude → guaranteed JSON response. Path B (Strict tool use): Request with tools[].strict: true → Claude → guaranteed valid tool_input. Both share the same engine: constrained decoding.
